name: NASTRAN
description: A foundational finite-element solver used throughout aerospace for structural analysis, modal response, buckling, and load-case evaluation.
whyUseIt: When aerospace teams need structural credibility, NASTRAN is still one of the default languages of the field. It is central to turning load cases into stress, deformation, and margin evidence.
engineerWorkflow: Create or import the structural idealisation, apply representative load cases, solve for stress and displacement, then interpret the result against margin criteria.
managerExpects: Traceable models, sensible assumptions, and analysts who can explain the physical story behind the contour plots.
demoDescription: Analyse a simplified wing-box or bracket under one manoeuvre load and identify the structural hot spot.

vocabulary.definition: A structural instability in which a component loses load-carrying ability through sudden deformation.
A measure of how far a structure is from its allowable limit under a given load case.

beginnerMistakes: Using unrealistic boundary conditions because they make the model easier
Reporting peak stress values without checking whether the model idealisation makes sense
